financial_diagnostic.title: Financial Diagnostic

financial_diagnostic.balanced_sheet: A balanced balance sheet with consistent data
financial_diagnostic.insufficient_data_desc: To generate a complete financial diagnostic, make sure you have imported the chart of accounts and accounting entries.
financial_diagnostic.no_critical_signal: No critical signal detected for this period. Continue monitoring with the comparative view.
financial_diagnostic.note_text: Diagnostic view controllable by mode, filters and comparatives. Premium exports reflect the current view (sort, filters, period, sector benchmark).
financial_diagnostic.customize_gallery_desc: Hide/reorder cards. Configuration is saved for your next visit.
financial_diagnostic.drilldown_desc: Details of accounts/sources related to this indicator.
financial_diagnostic.no_source_accounts: No accounting source available for this KPI.

revenue.title: Revenue
revenue.why: This KPI directly tracks the company's commercial traction.
revenue.how: Improve conversion, average basket and customer retention.
revenue.info_formula: Sum of net sales over the period
revenue.info_definition: Revenue measures the level of commercial activity.
revenue.info_utility: It serves as the basis for all performance ratios.
revenue.info_interpretation: A sustained increase is positive. A decrease requires client/price/volume analysis.

gross_margin.title: Gross margin
gross_margin.why: Gross margin shows the ability to convert sales into value.
gross_margin.how: Renegotiate purchases, adjust pricing, prioritize higher-contribution offerings.
gross_margin.info_definition: Gross margin shows value created before overhead costs.
gross_margin.info_utility: It validates the price/direct cost pairing.
gross_margin.info_interpretation: A decline often reflects pricing pressure or rising direct costs.

gross_margin_rate.title: Gross margin (%)
gross_margin_rate.why: The percentage makes margin comparable across periods.
gross_margin_rate.how: Improve product mix and purchasing efficiency.

ebitda.title: EBITDA
ebitda.why: EBITDA measures operational performance before non-cash items.
ebitda.how: Optimize recurring costs and team productivity.

ebitda_margin.title: EBITDA margin
ebitda_margin.why: This ratio shows the share of revenue converted into operating performance.
ebitda_margin.how: Automate processes, review discount policy, reduce fixed costs.

operating_result.title: Operating result
operating_result.why: It indicates whether the core business is profitable.
operating_result.how: Reallocate budget to profitable segments and control OPEX.

operating_margin.title: Operating margin
operating_margin.why: It provides a quick view of economic solidity.
operating_margin.how: Manage pricing, productivity and cost structure.

caf.title: Self-financing capacity (CFO)
caf.why: CFO indicates the internal resources generated by the business.
caf.how: Improve profitability and limit unproductive costs.

working_capital.title: Working capital
working_capital.why: Working capital represents the long-term financial safety margin.
working_capital.how: Strengthen equity or extend debt maturity.

bfr.title: Working capital requirement (WCR)
bfr.why: WCR measures cash tied up in the operating cycle.
bfr.how: Speed up collections, optimize inventory, negotiate with suppliers.

bfr_variation.title: WCR variation
bfr_variation.why: A positive variation can absorb available cash.
bfr_variation.how: Implement DSO/DPO tracking and inventory thresholds.

operating_cashflow.title: Operating cash flow
operating_cashflow.why: This flow validates the business's ability to generate real cash.
operating_cashflow.how: Increase CFO and reduce WCR consumption.

net_debt.title: Net debt
net_debt.why: It measures the real weight of debt after available cash.

debt_ratio.title: Debt ratio
debt_ratio.why: It measures the debt / equity weight.
debt_ratio.how: Limit short-term debt and strengthen equity.

debt_share.title: Debt share
debt_share.why: This indicator visualizes the level of dependency on debt leverage.
debt_share.how: Diversify financing and reduce circulating cash needs.

roe.title: ROE
roe.why: ROE measures the return on equity.
roe.how: Improve net margin and capital turnover.

roce.title: ROCE
roce.why: ROCE measures the performance of capital employed.

roa.title: ROA
roa.why: ROA links profitability and asset efficiency.
roa.how: Increase net income and optimize asset usage.

net_margin.title: Net margin
net_margin.why: Net margin gives the final profitability after all charges.
net_margin.how: Control financial/tax charges and indirect costs.

current_ratio.title: Current ratio
current_ratio.why: It measures the ability to honor short-term debts.
current_ratio.how: Strengthen cash and reduce short-term debt.

quick_ratio.title: Quick ratio
quick_ratio.why: It tests solvency without selling inventory.
quick_ratio.how: Better collect receivables and stabilize supplier payment terms.

cash_ratio.title: Cash ratio
cash_ratio.why: It measures the immediate cash coverage of short-term debts.
cash_ratio.how: Maintain cash buffer and weekly cash forecasts.

financial_leverage.title: Financial leverage
financial_leverage.why: High leverage increases sensitivity to repayment risk.
financial_leverage.how: Strengthen equity and reduce unproductive debt.

autonomy_ratio.title: Financial autonomy
autonomy_ratio.why: It measures the share of internal financing.
autonomy_ratio.how: Consolidate reserves and prudent management of external financing.

alerts.operating_margin_action: Review cost structure and pricing for low-contribution offerings.
alerts.current_ratio_action: Secure immediate cash and accelerate collections.
alerts.leverage_action: Prioritize debt reduction and targeted recapitalization.
alerts.cashflow_action: Stop non-essential outflows and correct WCR.
alerts.bfr_action: Monitor DSO, DPO and inventory turnover weekly.
alerts.ebitda_margin_action: Review operational processes and fixed costs.

pricing.title: CashPilot Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Choose the plan that fits your business. Start free, scale at your pace.
pricing.subscriptionDesc: Get credits every month at a discounted rate. Unused credits reset at each renewal.
pricing.creditPacksDesc: Need more? Buy one-time credit packs that never expire.
pricing.footer: All prices in EUR, excl. VAT. Subscriptions renew automatically. Cancel anytime.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Your subscription credits are credited each month on your renewal date. Unused credits do not roll over to the next month. Credits purchased as packs remain available indefinitely.
faq.q2: Can I change my plan at any time?
faq.a2: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time. The change takes effect immediately and the amount is prorated.
faq.a3: With annual billing, you pay the equivalent of 10 months instead of 12, saving 2 months. Payment is made as a single charge.
faq.q4: What happens if I exceed my monthly credits?
faq.a4: You can purchase additional credit packs at any time. These credits never expire and are used after your subscription credits.
faq.q5: How do I cancel my subscription?
faq.a5: You can cancel your subscription at any time from the billing settings. You keep access until the end of your paid period.

emptyStates.valuationUnavailableHint: No usable EBITDA and no positive free cash flow over the period: valuation stays intentionally conservative.

quality.blockedTitle: Business steering blocked by accounting anomalies
quality.warningTitle: Business steering should be reviewed before decision-making use
quality.blockedHint: Fix the chart of accounts or inconsistent journal entries first. The indicators shown should not be used for decision-making until these issues are resolved.
quality.warningHint: The dataset remains usable, but some classifications or journal entries should be reviewed before considering the steering layer fully reliable.

guide.title: Business steering usage guide
guide.subtitle: Read this before relying on ratios, tax outputs, or valuation.
guide.summary: CashPilot business steering consolidates accounting entries, journals generated from business flows, and FR / BE / OHADA settings to produce a management view. The figures are useful for steering a company only when the accounting dataset is clean, complete, and reviewed whenever the application flags a risk.
guide.currentStatusHelp: This status tells you whether the dataset is ready, requires review, or should be blocked before decision-making use.
guide.currentValuationHelp: Valuation depends on the quality of the earnings, cash-flow, and accounting data available over the selected period.

functioning.title: What the steering layer currently does
functioning.intro: The engine computes all tabs from a single accounting base and a single analysis period.
points: All 6 tabs share the same period, accounting region, and sector so that the outputs remain consistent.
KPIs, ratios, alerts, tax summaries, and valuation are recomputed whenever the period or source data changes.
Indicators are driven first by accounting data and journalized flows, not by marketing inputs or simple estimates.

data.title: What data is actually used
data.intro: The steering layer is only as reliable as the data that feeds it.
points: An initialized chart of accounts, with accounts correctly classified by type and category.
Dated, balanced entries linked to existing accounts and free of inconsistent accounting standards mixing.
Journals generated from invoices, expenses, payments, receipts, and disbursements when those flows exist in the file.
The selected FR / Belgium / OHADA zone and sector, used for analysis rules and benchmarks.

conditions.title: Recommended conditions of use
conditions.intro: Use the steering layer as a management tool only when these conditions are met.
points: Prefer a dataset flagged as "dataset ready for analysis". If the status is warning, review the issues. If it is blocked, do not use the steering layer for decisions.
For first-time use on a real company, reconcile at least revenue, net income, equity, financial debt, and working capital with the accountant's figures.
Work on a complete period that matches the decision you are making: closed month, closed quarter, or explicitly identified current year.
Check the currency, accounting region, and sector before interpreting ratios, tax outputs, or valuation.

limits.title: What the steering layer does not replace
limits.intro: The module supports management decisions; it does not replace legal obligations or professional judgment.
points: It does not replace a statutory close, tax filing, corporate return, or legal audit.
A DCF or multiples valuation remains a management estimate, not a binding transaction or investment price.
If the dataset is partial, taken over mid-year, heavily reclassified, or still being cleaned, the indicators must be reviewed by an accountant.
When in doubt on a sensitive ratio, trust the source data and accounting review before trusting a single chart.

dataRequirements.allReadyTitle: All analyses in this tab are backed by available data
dataRequirements.allReadyBody: The sections below have the minimum database inputs required to produce reliable outputs.
dataRequirements.summaryBody: CashPilot only shows analyses when the required database inputs exist. The sections below list what is still missing.
dataRequirements.partialBody: Part of this analysis can be computed, but some results or charts remain limited because the database inputs are still incomplete.
dataRequirements.unavailableBody: This analysis is hidden because the database does not yet contain all the inputs required to produce a reliable result.

mcp.description: Control CashPilot in natural language from your AI assistant through remote SSE.

chatgpt.description: Core URL. Compatible with ChatGPT limits.

security.title: Security
security.description: The URL contains a secret key. Do not share it.

connector.title: MCP Connector — Anthropic API
connector.description: Connect to CashPilot from the Anthropic Messages API without a local install.

steps.step1: Generate a CashPilot API key in the REST API section below.
steps.step2: Add the MCP server to your Anthropic call with mcp_servers.
steps.step3: Use mcp_toolset so Claude can access CashPilot tools automatically.
steps.step4: Claude can then manage invoices, clients, and payments in natural language.
